connection.note: extract-based - original logical source was 'excel'; actual rows come from the packaged .hyper file, not a live connection

tables.name: TableauSQL
tables.custom_sql:
SELECT
  1 as [path order],
  'source' as [link position],
  [source].[Availability (%)] AS [Availability (%)],
  [source].[BH Survey Method] AS [BH Survey Method],
  [source].[Cand ID Source] AS [Cand ID],
  [source].[Capacity (Mb/s)] AS [Capacity (Mb/s)],
  [source].[Distance (miles)] AS [Distance (miles)],
  [source].[Forecast Decision] AS [Forecast Decision],
  [source].[Freq Band (GHz)] AS [Freq Band (GHz)],
  [source].[Lat Source] AS [Lat],
  [source].[Link ID] AS [Link ID],
  [source].[Link Status] AS [Link Status],
  [source].[Link Type] AS [Link Type],
  [source].[Long Source] AS [Long],
  [source].[Polarization Source] AS [Polarization],
  [source].[Radio Model] AS [Radio Model],
  [source].[Site ID Source] AS [Site ID],
  [source].[Site Status Source] AS [Site Status],
  [source].[Site Type BH Source] AS [Site Type BH],
  [source].[SLA] AS [SLA],
  [source].[Survey Date] AS [Survey Date],
  [source].[Survey Status] AS [Survey Status],
  [source].[TX Type Source] AS [TX Type],
  [source].[Vendor] AS [Vendor]
FROM [celldata$] [source]
UNION
SELECT
  2 as [path order],
  'target' as [link position],
  [target].[Availability (%)] AS [Availability (%)],
  [target].[BH Survey Method] AS [BH Survey Method],
  [target].[Cand ID Target] AS [Cand ID],
  [target].[Capacity (Mb/s)] AS [Capacity (Mb/s)],
  [target].[Distance (miles)] AS [Distance (miles)],
  [target].[Forecast Decision] AS [Forecast Decision],
  [target].[Freq Band (GHz)] AS [Freq Band (GHz)],
  [target].[Lat Target] AS [Lat],
  [target].[Link ID] AS [Link ID],
  [target].[Link Status] AS [Link Status],
  [target].[Link Type] AS [Link Type],
  [target].[Long Target] AS [Long],
  [target].[Polarization Target] AS [Polarization],
  [target].[Radio Model] AS [Radio Model],
  [target].[Site ID Target] AS [Site ID],
  [target].[Site Status Target] AS [Site Status],
  [target].[Site Type BH Target] AS [Site Type BH],
  [target].[SLA] AS [SLA],
  [target].[Survey Date] AS [Survey Date],
  [target].[Survey Status] AS [Survey Status],
  [target].[TX Type Target] AS [TX Type],
  [target].[Vendor] AS [Vendor]
FROM [celldata$] [target]

theme.source_note: Tableau has no single global theme file - aggregated from per-worksheet mark-color formats; treat as a starting palette, not an authoritative theme to clone.

limitations_encountered.issue: extract-based (.hyper) data source - row data requires a separate extraction step (tableauhyperapi -> Parquet) or repointing to the true upstream system
could not resolve shelf reference UNRESOLVED:Regional Group (group)

Tableau 'Measure Names/Measure Values' virtual pivot - no direct Power BI equivalent. Bind each resolved field in pivoted_field_ids directly as its own field on the target visual (e.g. one Y-axis field per measure on a clustered column chart), rather than trying to recreate a literal pivot column. (axis: columns, resolved fields: 4)
could not resolve shelf reference UNRESOLVED:Regional Group (group)

Extract-based .hyper source materialized to data/ds.cell_links.csv (808 rows x 24 cols) and loaded as an import-mode table via Csv.Document with explicit en-US culture on Table.TransformColumnTypes. This is a structure-preserving migration against extracted data; repoint to the live upstream if one exists behind the extract before production use.

Tableau table is a custom-SQL UNION stacking each link's source endpoint (link position='source', path order=1) and target endpoint (path order=2): 404 links x 2 = 808 rows. The UNION is already baked into the extract CSV, so it is modeled as one flat import table (grain = link endpoint), not reconstructed in Power Query.

Geographic coordinates typed as Double (deliberate exception to the avoid-Double modeling guideline) to preserve source precision, with dataCategory Latitude/Longitude for native map binding and summarizeBy none.

Tableau manual group is PARTIAL: only 240 of 808 rows (7 named regions: South, South West, Central East, Central West, North, North East, North West) are grouped; the other 568 rows were left ungrouped by the author and render as their raw rounded 'lat-long' coordinate label. Reproduced as a DAX calc column: SWITCH over integer lat*10/long*10 (exact HALF_UP == Tableau ROUND), with an ungrouped FORMAT fallback. Named/ungrouped split (240/568) and per-region capacity share validated in fabric/_validation/groundtruth_check.py.

limitations_encountered.item: Regional Group / Rounded Lat / Regional Grouping / Rounded Long / Regional Grouping (group)
limitations_encountered.issue: Calc-field dispositions: 'Regional Group' (internal_name [Rounded Lat (copy)], formula str(ROUND([Lat],1))+str(ROUND([Long],1))) CONSOLIDATED into the Regional Group (group) calc-column definition (its only purpose was to feed the group). EXCLUDED as vestigial/unreferenced: 'Rounded Lat' (ROUND([Lat],2)), 'Regional Grouping' (internal_name [Rounded Long], ROUND([Long],2)), and the empty group 'Regional Grouping (group)'. Note stale internal names (caption != internal_name) confirmed here - always resolve by caption.

Aggregated Tableau shelf pills mapped to 7 explicit DAX measures: Total Capacity (sum), Avg Capacity (avg), % of Total Capacity, Avg Availability (avg), Avg Freq Band (avg), Total Distance (sum), and hidden Number of Records (COUNTROWS). The 4 raw numeric base columns (Availability (%), Capacity (Mb/s), Distance (miles), Freq Band (GHz)) are hidden with summarizeBy none so consumers use the measures. All 6 aggregates ground-truth verified (MATCH) in fabric/_validation/groundtruth_check.py.

Tableau table calc pcto:sum:Capacity (Mb/s) ('Percent of Total') translated to DIVIDE([Total Capacity], CALCULATE([Total Capacity], ALLSELECTED('Cell Links'))). Denominator = grand total across the visual selection (validated to equal 115212). Table-calc addressing/partitioning should be re-checked against Tableau if a live comparison becomes available.

Tableau 'Number of Records' calc field (formula: 1) modeled as a hidden COUNTROWS measure; not bound to any worksheet shelf in this workbook (kept for parity / forward use, matching prior builds' convention).

Excluded from the model: 10 fk:* and 10 pk:* hidden Tableau internal group-index columns (absent from the extract CSV) and the ':Measure Names' pseudo-dimension. These are Tableau-internal plumbing with no Power BI equivalent.
Typed as dateTime from ISO 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss' strings (only 54 of 808 rows populated) with explicit en-US culture. Verify date parse on live refresh (no live engine available at build time).
The 4 pivoted measures for the report builder to bind individually (Measure Names/Values has no native PBI equivalent): Total Capacity, Avg Availability, Avg Freq Band, Total Distance.

The frozen calc column 'Regional Group (group)' resolves to 27 distinct row values (7 named regions + 20 coordinate-label fallbacks using 1-decimal longitude: 47.5-122.0 .. 47.8-122.6), whereas the Tableau reference screenshot shows only 11 (7 named + 4 integer-longitude labels like '47.5-122'). The calc column faithfully reproduces the workbook author's PARTIAL manual group (validated 240 grouped / 568 ungrouped by the semantic build), so the reference screenshot reflects a different/earlier grouping state or integer-rounded longitude display. Cannot be reconciled without modifying the frozen model. Overview bar therefore renders 27 segments/bars rather than 11.

Handoff requested the segment label = '% of Total Capacity'. That measure uses ALLSELECTED('Cell Links') as a GRAND-TOTAL denominator (per semantic build), so a per-segment (Regional Group x Link Status) value yields sub-1% figures, not a readable per-bar share, and the Tableau reference labels are absolute Mb/s. DEVIATION: bound the segment data label to the absolute 'Total Capacity' value (matches reference) and moved '% of Total Capacity' + 'Avg Capacity' into the visual tooltip instead.

Tableau encodes link capacity as LINE THICKNESS on the microwave-link path marks. The Power BI azureMap pathLayer strokeWidth is a single scalar and cannot be data-bound to a Size role. FIDELITY GAP: capacity is instead bound to the bubbleLayer Size role (endpoint bubble radius). Native azureMap DID reproduce the 2-endpoint line map itself via PathID (Link ID) + PointOrder (path order) + pathLayer (a fidelity win over Tableau's dual-axis workaround).

Two deviations: (1) the 'Regional Group (group)' row header present on Tableau's rows shelf is OMITTED from the matrix (its header was hidden in the source worksheet + it is the 27-group problem field); rows = [Link ID, Site ID] reproduces the screenshot's two-endpoint merged pattern (e.g. one Link ID -> two Site IDs). (2) Tableau colors the numeric text green/olive by Link Status; per-cell text color driven by an off-table dimension is not achievable against the frozen model, so numbers render in the default theme color.

'Total Capacity' uses the frozen model formatString '#,##0' so a value renders as e.g. '54' where the Tableau reference shows '54.0' (one decimal). Trivial format-only gap; not changed because the semantic model is frozen.

Tableau dashboard filter actions on Link Status and Regional Group are reproduced via native Power BI cross-highlighting (drillFilterOtherVisuals) rather than helper slicer visuals, per handoff. No explicit slicers exist on the page, so no slicer-default gotcha applies.

azureMap requires the Category (Location) role AND was given explicit Lat/Long overrides; Power BI Desktop rendered a visual error ('Remove Location to display latitude and longitude pairs, or set the aggregate for Latitude and Longitude to Average'). Structural validation did NOT catch this (schema-valid). FIX: kept Site ID in Category (Location) and converted the Lat (Y) and Long (X) projections to Average aggregations (Aggregation.Function=1). Verified lossless: every (Link ID, path order) endpoint grain maps to exactly one coordinate (808/808), so Avg over the visual grain returns the exact coordinate. Re-verified in Desktop: error glyph gone, azureMap renders.

Power BI Desktop (Microsoft Store/MSIX build 2.156.879.0) became available mid-build (launched by a parallel report build). Opened this report in its OWN new instance (pid 11444, via PBI_DESKTOP_PATH to the WindowsApps exe) without disrupting the concurrent build. STRUCTURAL validation CLEAN and the Desktop screenshot caught + fixed a real azureMap binding error (see Location vs Lat/Long entry). REMAINING GAP: the import model shows 'calculated columns need to be manually refreshed' + 'tables have incomplete or no data', so bars/matrix rows/map points render EMPTY. The Desktop Bridge manifest exposes only application.state.get, report.snapshot.capture, and file.reload -- NO data-refresh method -- so a headless refresh is not possible. DataFolder resolves to a valid local CSV (data/ds.cell_links.csv, 808 rows), so data will populate on a manual 'Refresh now'. Data-populated visual verification (bar segments, matrix rows, map path lines, legend color+size) therefore remains PENDING a manual refresh.
